description: A Southern American dish of shrimp served over grits, often with a savory sauce that may include butter, bacon, stock, cheese, garlic, or peppers. Styles range from simple coastal preparations to richer restaurant versions.
preparation_steps: Cook the grits until soft and creamy.
Season and cook the shrimp quickly until just done.
Prepare any sauce or sautéed aromatics such as garlic, bacon, or peppers.
Spoon the grits onto a plate or into a bowl.
Top with shrimp and sauce and serve hot.

nutrition.fat: Varies from low to high depending on butter, bacon, and cheese

health_advice.benefits: Provides protein from shrimp and can be satisfying as a one-plate meal.
health_advice.risks: Restaurant versions may be high in sodium, butter, cheese, or processed pork.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use moderate butter and cheese, and add vegetables for a more balanced plate.

dietary_notes.note: Usually non-vegetarian. Often gluten-free if the sauce and seasonings do not contain gluten.

allergens: Dairy if butter or cheese is used
